text:
#### 4. Fraud Detection Engine (FDE)  
**System ID:** FDE-001
**Purpose:** Real-time detection and prevention of fraudulent transactions
**Technology:** Python, Apache Kafka, TensorFlow (ML models)
**Deployment:** Cloud (AWS) + on-premises (for sensitive rules)
**Latency:** <100ms per transaction  
**Key Functions:**
- Transaction risk scoring
- Anomaly detection (ML models)
- Rule-based detection
- Card fraud prevention
- Account takeover detection
- Suspicious activity flagging  
**Fraud Rules (Examples):**
- Large transaction amount (>£5,000)
- Geographic anomaly (transaction in different country within 2 hours)
- Velocity checks (>5 transactions within 1 hour)
- Unusual beneficiary (new beneficiary + large transfer)
- Card not present (CNP) high-value transaction  
**Machine Learning Models:**
- Transaction Risk Model (trained on 2,000+ fraud cases)
- Anomaly Detection Model (unsupervised)
- Chargeback Prediction Model  
**Alert Escalation:**
- Low Risk: Auto-approve
- Medium Risk: Require OTP verification
- High Risk: Block transaction, notify customer, escalate to Financial Crime team (see [[departments]])  
**Performance:**
- Detection Rate: 97%+
- False Positive Rate: <2%
- Monthly fraud prevented: ~£45k–£80k  
**Integration:**
- Account Management System (AMS) — transaction data
- Internet Banking Platform (IBP) — real-time blocking
- Mobile Banking App (MBA)
- Payment Processing System (PPS)
- Financial Crime System — SAR filing  
---
name: Chunk 7/39

raw_entities.name: Fraud Detection Engine
raw_entities.description: A real-time transaction risk scoring and anomaly detection platform deployed across AWS cloud and on-premises infrastructure. The system leverages Python, Apache Kafka, and TensorFlow machine learning models to evaluate transaction velocity, geographic patterns, and beneficiary data. It serves as the central control point for preventing card fraud, account takeovers, and suspicious activity flagging before transactions are finalized.
context: Real-time detection and prevention of fraudulent transactions

raw_entities.name: Account Management System
raw_entities.description: The primary core banking system responsible for maintaining customer account data and transaction records. It feeds raw transaction data to the Fraud Detection Engine to enable real-time risk scoring and anomaly detection workflows.

raw_entities.name: Internet Banking Platform
raw_entities.description: The web-based customer channel integrated with the Fraud Detection Engine to enforce real-time transaction blocking. It acts as the execution layer that halts suspicious payments identified by FDE risk models.

raw_entities.name: Mobile Banking App
raw_entities.description: The mobile application channel connected to the Fraud Detection Engine for continuous transaction monitoring and security enforcement across mobile devices.

raw_entities.name: Payment Processing System
raw_entities.description: The backend payment infrastructure that routes and settles customer transactions. It integrates with FDE to ensure all payment executions are screened against fraud rules before settlement.

raw_entities.name: Financial Crime System
raw_entities.description: The dedicated compliance platform used to generate and file Suspicious Activity Reports (SARs). It receives high-risk fraud alerts escalated from the FDE for regulatory documentation and investigation tracking.

raw_entities.name: Financial Crime team
raw_entities.description: An internal governance and operational unit responsible for investigating high-risk fraud alerts escalated by the Fraud Detection Engine. The team manages SAR filing coordination, customer notification protocols, and advanced transaction blocking decisions.

raw_entities.name: Transaction Latency Threshold
raw_entities.description: The maximum allowable processing time for the Fraud Detection Engine to evaluate and score each transaction. This SLA ensures real-time risk assessment without disrupting customer payment flows, capped at under 100 milliseconds per transaction.

raw_entities.name: Fraud Detection Rate
raw_entities.description: A key performance indicator measuring the system's accuracy in identifying actual fraudulent activity. The target requires a minimum detection rate of 97% or higher to maintain effective fraud prevention standards.

raw_entities.name: False Positive Rate Limit
raw_entities.description: An operational constraint limiting the frequency of incorrectly flagged legitimate transactions. The system must maintain a false positive rate below 2% to prevent unnecessary customer friction and operational overhead.

raw_entities.name: Monthly Fraud Prevention Value
raw_entities.description: A financial KPI tracking the monetary value of fraudulent transactions successfully blocked by the FDE. The target range indicates monthly savings between £45,000 and £80,000 through proactive risk intervention.

raw_entities.name: Large Transaction Amount Rule
raw_entities.description: A hard operational constraint that flags any single transaction exceeding £5,000 for enhanced risk scoring and potential manual review within the FDE workflow.

raw_entities.name: Geographic Anomaly Rule
raw_entities.description: A detection rule that triggers alerts when a transaction occurs in a different country within a 2-hour window, identifying potential card skimming or account takeover attempts.
context: Geographic anomaly (transaction in different country within 2 hours)

raw_entities.name: Transaction Velocity Check
raw_entities.description: An operational limit that flags accounts executing more than 5 transactions within a single hour, preventing rapid sequential fraud or automated bot attacks.

raw_entities.name: Unusual Beneficiary Rule
raw_entities.description: A risk control that flags transfers directed to newly added beneficiaries combined with large transfer amounts, mitigating social engineering and authorized push payment fraud.

raw_entities.name: Card Not Present High-Value Rule
raw_entities.description: A specialized detection rule targeting high-value transactions conducted without physical card presence, requiring additional scrutiny due to elevated fraud exposure.

raw_entities.name: Low Risk Escalation Protocol
raw_entities.description: A governance rule dictating that transactions classified as low risk are automatically approved without requiring manual intervention or additional verification steps.

raw_entities.name: Medium Risk Verification Rule
raw_entities.description: An operational requirement mandating One-Time Password (OTP) verification for medium-risk flagged transactions to confirm customer intent before authorization.

raw_entities.name: High Risk Blocking Protocol
raw_entities.description: A hard control requiring immediate transaction blocking, automated customer notification, and escalation to the Financial Crime team when high-risk fraud indicators are detected.
context: High Risk: Block transaction, notify customer, escalate to Financial Crime team

raw_entities.name: Transaction
raw_entities.description: The core banking entity representing monetary exchanges evaluated by the Fraud Detection Engine. Transactions are subjected to risk scoring, velocity checks, geographic anomaly detection, and rule-based screening before settlement.
context: Real-time detection and prevention of fraudulent transactions

raw_entities.name: Card
raw_entities.description: A payment instrument monitored for fraud prevention within the FDE framework. The system specifically tracks card-not-present (CNP) high-value transactions and physical usage anomalies to prevent unauthorized card exploitation.

raw_entities.name: Account
raw_entities.description: A customer financial record subject to account takeover detection and continuous risk scoring. The FDE monitors account behavior patterns to identify unauthorized access or suspicious activity flagging.

raw_entities.name: SAR
raw_entities.description: A regulatory reporting document generated and filed through the Financial Crime System. SARs are triggered by high-risk fraud alerts identified by the FDE to satisfy anti-money laundering and financial crime compliance obligations.

raw_entities.name: Fraud Alert
raw_entities.description: A system-generated notification indicating potential fraudulent activity, categorized into low, medium, or high risk tiers. Alerts dictate specific escalation protocols, from auto-approval to transaction blocking and Financial Crime team review.

properties.purpose: Real-time detection and prevention of fraudulent transactions

properties.what_it_measures: Percentage of fraudulent transactions successfully identified
Rate of legitimate transactions incorrectly flagged as fraud
Total monetary value of fraudulent activity stopped monthly

properties.rule_statement: Flag transactions occurring in a different country within 2 hours of previous transaction
Flag more than 5 transactions within a 1-hour window
Flag transfers to new beneficiaries combined with large amounts
Flag high-value transactions where the physical card is not presented
Automatically approve transactions flagged as low risk
Require One-Time Password verification for medium risk alerts
Block transaction, notify customer, and escalate to Financial Crime team for high risk alerts

relationships.description: FDE feeds transaction data to AMS for risk scoring and validation
FDE enables real-time transaction blocking on the IBP interface
FDE connects to the mobile app to validate transactions and push fraud alerts
FDE interfaces with PPS to score payment risks before settlement
FDE transmits flagged suspicious activity data to the Financial Crime System for SAR filing
FDE performs real-time risk scoring and anomaly detection on individual transactions
relationships.context: Real-time detection and prevention of fraudulent transactions / Transaction risk scoring

relationships.description: FDE oversees card fraud prevention workflows and validates card-based transactions
FDE monitors suspicious activity and generates Fraud Alerts for escalation
Financial Crime System records and archives SAR filings generated from FDE alerts
FDE performance is quantified against the transaction latency threshold
FDE detection capabilities are tracked against the fraud detection rate metric
FDE output precision is monitored against the false positive rate limit
FDE tracks the financial value of fraud prevented on a monthly basis
FDE automatically enforces the large transaction amount rule during risk scoring
FDE applies the geographic anomaly rule to detect cross-border fraud patterns
relationships.context: Geographic anomaly (transaction in different country within 2 hours)

relationships.description: FDE executes velocity checks to prevent rapid sequential fraudulent transactions
FDE flags transfers involving new beneficiaries combined with large amounts
FDE applies the CNP high-value rule to validate non-face-to-face card transactions
FDE escalates high-risk fraud alerts to the Financial Crime team for manual review and action
relationships.context: High Risk: Block transaction, notify customer, escalate to Financial Crime team

relationships.description: The Financial Crime team is accountable for the monthly fraud prevention value metric
